content.problemStatement:
There is an ATM machine. Initially, it contains a total of KKK units of money. NNN people (numbered 111 through NNN) want to withdraw money; for each valid iii, the iii-th person wants to withdraw AiA_iAi​ units of money.

The people come in and try to withdraw money one by one, in the increasing order of their indices. Whenever someone tries to withdraw money, if the machine has at least the required amount of money, it will give out the required amount. Otherwise, it will throw an error and not give out anything; in that case, this person will return home directly without trying to do anything else.

For each person, determine whether they will get the required amount of money or not.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and K. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing a string with length N. For each valid i, the i-th character of this string should be '1' if the i-th person will successfully withdraw their money or '0' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the ATM initially has 10 units. The first person withdraws 3 units, leaving 7 units. The second person withdraws 5 units, leaving 2 units. The third person cannot withdraw 3 units because there are only 2 units left, so they get 0. The fourth person withdraws the remaining 2 units, leaving no balance. The fifth person cannot withdraw any money because the ATM is out of balance, so they get 0. For the second test case, the ATM initially has 6 units. The first person cannot withdraw 10 units because there are only 6 units available, so they get 0. The second person also cannot withdraw 8 units for the same reason, so they get 0. The third person can withdraw 6 units, leaving no balance. The fourth person cannot withdraw any money because the ATM is out of balance, so they get 0.

solution.approach: The approach to solve this problem is to iterate through each person's withdrawal request in order. For each person, check if the ATM has enough money to fulfill their request. If it does, subtract the requested amount from the ATM's total and mark the person as successful (1). If it doesn't, mark the person as unsuccessful (0). This process continues until all people have been processed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum number of people in a test case. This is because for each test case, we potentially iterate through all N people.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — for storing the result string of length N for each test case.
